theorem: MDHG geometric cache as discrete-continuous bridge: quantize retraction of the continuous 24D Leech space onto a discrete slot lattice, stabilized by SpeedLight idempotence f(f(x)) = f(x)
adjudicated_divergence: quantize() made total on all reals (input clamped into [0,1) before floor); source assumed inputs already in [0,1)
eviction made deterministic via a monotonic admission sequence (source used wall-clock time, non-deterministic under equal hits)
CA-field self-regulating dynamics (CAField, kernel_step, Wolfram assignments) stay product-side; the forge carries the proven cache + idempotence core
tower_link: the 24 dimensions are the Leech dimension; the bridge addresses points of the continuous Leech space constructed exactly in LeechForge (CQE-paper-17)
open_obligations: The CA-field dynamics need their own stability receipt before promotion (Wolfram-class kernels).
Slot collision rate vs the double-hash distribution is an empirical product diagnostic, not a paper claim.
